text: Is it possible to do the 3d gel like structure in a home enviroment? considering the transparency , having multiple layers stacked would help in any way? would it be possible to apply the transparent coating onto existing non removable house/ apartment windows and get some electricity out of them without losing the incoming light and make it somehow stick to the glass/plastic as it is in it's vertical position?

author: @lisasis2c235
text: What an interesting dynamic...I wonder how long the improvements last and if they are changed by temperature?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: temperature doesn't really have a noticeable effect

replies.author: @lisasis2c235
replies.text: Are there other forces of outdoor applications that might change effectiveness

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
lisasis2c
Probably - it would certainly be something to experiment on - let me know how you get on if you do look at this

replies.author: @lisasis2c235
replies.text: Well thanks for the vote of confidence.  I'll be glad to look into it as soon as I fix a few things in my immediate field of need.  Currently the washing machine, and a few less mechanical doings.

author: @helpmonkey
text: it would be interesting to see if drying the ink under vibration would improve performance?? the diamagnetic particles should form more properly polarized chains improving electron transport and may also improve the electrode barrier effect?? Also if the vibration frequency could be made resonant with the nano particles further improvement might result?

author: @actudoran
text: groovy stuff !!! gave me quite a bunch of ideeas ... on to playing with my hallbach arrays :))

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: something everyone should play with lol

replies.author: @actudoran
replies.text:
Too right  ... I got 60 magnets N50 that promise hours of fun ... don't want to use super glue and hot glue is out of question ... will build a 3 d printer and stuff them in preset shapes ... or that's the plan ... till then I'll just sacrifice those and glue them accordingly ... 
Been today to the inventika exhibition in Bucharest and saw a few things ... but as usual the good stuff is expensive as hell ... 
No 3d printers there ... and the energy products were way conservative ... photo polarisations sprays were in the forefront and cogeneration equipment was yhere too ...

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
actudoran
still probably pretty interesting mate

replies.author: @actudoran
replies.text: Yup my two little ones enjoyed it too ... had a look at a few laser and plasma cutters as well as some ultrasonic welding machines ... no pics allowed ... a magnet motor was there too consuming 12 v and about 5 amps ...  with free spining stator magnets and 5 discs of magnets

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
actudoran
that i would have liked to see
